prompt: Add a remote MCP server named 'sentry' to my opencode.json. It should connect to https://mcp.sentry.io/mcp, use OAuth authentication, and have a 10-second timeout. Show me the exact JSON to add.

assertions.description: Sets type to 'remote'
Uses url field with https://mcp.sentry.io/mcp
Does not set oauth: false (leaves it for auto or provides OAuth config)
Sets timeout to 10000 (milliseconds)
Entry is nested under top-level 'mcp' object with key 'sentry'
Includes enabled: true or omits it (not sets to false)

prompt: I want to use GitLab Duo as an AI provider in opencode. How do I configure it? I'm using a self-hosted GitLab instance at https://gitlab.mycompany.com. Include the environment variable setup.

assertions.description: Names the provider as 'gitlab' or equivalent
References GITLAB_TOKEN environment variable
References GITLAB_INSTANCE_URL or equivalent for self-hosted
Uses {env:VARIABLE_NAME} syntax for secrets in config
Shows the provider block inside the 'provider' object in opencode.json

prompt: Create a custom opencode agent called 'auditor' that: is a subagent, uses claude-sonnet-4-5, has read-only permissions (can read files but cannot edit or run bash), and has a temperature of 0.1. Show me both the JSON config and the equivalent markdown agent file.

assertions.description: Sets mode to 'subagent'
Sets model to anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-5 or similar
Sets permission.edit to 'deny'
Sets permission.bash to 'deny'
Sets permission.read to 'allow'
Sets temperature to 0.1
Shows YAML frontmatter format for the .md agent file
Mentions ~/.config/opencode/agents/ or .opencode/agents/ as the file location

prompt: How do I change the leader key in opencode from the default to ctrl+space? Also, how do I disable the session_fork keybind entirely? Which config file do these go in?

assertions.description: Identifies tui.json (not opencode.json) as the target file
Gives correct path ~/.config/opencode/tui.json
Shows keybinds.leader: 'ctrl+space' or equivalent
Shows setting session_fork to empty string '' or 'none' to disable
Shows both changes nested inside a 'keybinds' object
